adult.default: 500 mg PO TID or 875 mg PO BID

pediatric.notes: AOM/sinusitis high-dose 80-90 mg/kg/d divided BID; GAS 50 mg/kg/d daily or divided BID, max 1 g

amoxicillin.notes: First-line for GAS, AOM, sinusitis, dental prophylaxis, Lyme. No anaerobic or Pseudomonas activity.

adult.high_dose: 2000/125 mg ER PO BID (high-dose for resistant pneumococcus)

pediatric.notes: AOM 90 mg/kg/d (amox component) divided BID; use ES-600 to limit clavulanate

amoxicillin-clavulanate.notes: Diarrhea common (clavulanate). Covers oral anaerobes, MSSA, beta-lactamase H. flu/Moraxella.

ampicillin.notes: Enterococcus, Listeria, susceptible E. coli, Proteus.

adult.crab_high_dose: Up to 27 g/d (sulbactam component 9-12 g/d) for CRAB

ampicillin-sulbactam.notes: High E. coli resistance rates; less reliable for IAI in 2025. CRAB high-dose regimens used.

penicillin-g.notes: Viridans strep IE, Listeria, syphilis (neuro), N. meningitidis.

pediatric.notes: Single IM for GAS pharyngitis if unable to take PO

benzathine-penicillin-g.notes: Do NOT use for neurosyphilis (insufficient CSF levels). Confirm Bicillin LA — not Bicillin C-R.

nafcillin.hepatic: Reduce dose 50% with severe hepatic + renal impairment
nafcillin.notes: MSSA. Hepatically cleared. Phlebitis common — central line preferred. AKI reported.

oxacillin.notes: Equivalent to nafcillin. Hepatotoxicity > nafcillin in some series.

dicloxacillin.notes: MSSA outpatient. Take on empty stomach.

renal.dose: 3.375 g IV q6h or 4.5 g IV q6h (severe)
3.375 g IV q8h (or 2.25 g if not severe)
2.25 g IV q8h; HD: 2.25 g q12h + 0.75 g post-HD

piperacillin-tazobactam.notes: Extended infusion improves PK. Avoid pip-tazo + vancomycin combo if AKI risk (controversial — recent data suggests less risk than feared).

pivmecillinam.notes: FDA approved 2024 for uncomplicated UTI in adult women. Active vs E. coli, Proteus, S. saprophyticus.

cefazolin.notes: MSSA workhorse. Surgical prophylaxis. Pre-op redosing if procedure >4h.

cephalexin.notes: MSSA, GAS, susceptible E. coli/Proteus/Klebsiella. PO MSSA SSTI.

cefuroxime.notes: H. flu, M. catarrhalis, mild Lyme.

cefoxitin.notes: Anaerobic coverage (B. fragilis). Surgical prophylaxis for colorectal/abdominal.

adult.gc: 500 mg IM ×1 (1 g IM if ≥150 kg)

pediatric.neonatal_caution: Avoid in neonates <28d if hyperbilirubinemic; do NOT mix with calcium-containing solutions

ceftriaxone.hepatic: If both renal+hepatic impairment, max 2 g/day
ceftriaxone.notes: Workhorse 3rd-gen. CAP, meningitis, pyelo, gonorrhea, IE (vgs/Strep). Biliary pseudolithiasis.

cefotaxime.notes: Equivalent spectrum to ceftriaxone. Preferred for neonatal sepsis (no calcium/bilirubin issue).

cefepime.notes: Neurotoxicity (encephalopathy, NCSE) at higher doses + renal impairment — adjust carefully. AmpC stable.

ceftaroline.notes: Active vs MRSA, MSSA, S. pneumo, GAS. Used for MRSA bacteremia salvage, refractory IE.

renal.dose: 750 mg load then 150 mg q8h; HD: 750 mg load then 150 mg q8h on HD days post-HD

ceftolozane-tazobactam.notes: First-line for DTR-Pseudomonas per IDSA AMR 2024. Not active vs MBL or KPC.

ceftazidime-avibactam.notes: Active vs KPC, OXA-48, ESBL, AmpC, DTR-Pseudomonas. NOT active vs MBL alone — combine with aztreonam.

renal.dose: 0.75 g IV q12h; HD: 0.75 g q12h, dose immediately post-HD

cefiderocol.notes: Active vs MBL, OXA, KPC, AmpC, MDR Pseudomonas, S. maltophilia, CRAB. Higher mortality signal in CREDIBLE-CR vs BAT — interpret with caution.

renal.dose: 500 mg q24h; HD: 500 mg q24h, give post-HD

ertapenem.notes: ESBL, ESBL pyelo. NOT active vs Pseudomonas, Acinetobacter, Enterococcus.

renal.dose: 500 mg q24h; HD: 500 mg q24h post-HD

meropenem.notes: Lower seizure risk than imipenem. Workhorse for resistant gram-negative.

renal.dose: Not recommended unless HD; 250 mg q12h on HD

imipenem-cilastatin.notes: Higher seizure risk than meropenem; lower threshold in renal impairment, CNS pathology, elderly.

imipenem-relebactam.notes: KPC-CRE, DTR-Pseudomonas. Same imipenem seizure considerations.

aztreonam.notes: Safe in PCN-allergy except known ceftazidime cross-reactivity. Gram-negative only — no gram-positive or anaerobic activity.

adult.default: Loading 6 g IV (over 3h), then 1.5 g IV q6h (3h infusion)

aztreonam-avibactam.notes: FDA approved Feb 2025 for cIAI with limited options. MBL CRE coverage.

adult.default: 1 g sulbactam + 1 g durlobactam IV q6h (3h infusion)

sulbactam-durlobactam.notes: FDA 2023 for HAP/VAP caused by Acinetobacter baumannii-calcoaceticus. Combine with imipenem or meropenem in label.

adult.default: AUC-targeted (400-600 mg·h/L) per IDSA 2020 — use vancomyzer.com for individualized dosing

renal.dose: Load 25 mg/kg then dose by levels; HD: redose post-HD by level

vancomycin.notes: Dose by AUC, not trough alone. Use vancomyzer.com for AUC-guided Bayesian dosing. Nephrotoxicity, infusion reactions, ototoxicity at high levels.

adult.fulminant: 500 mg PO/NG QID; 500 mg in 100 mL NS PR q6h if ileus

vancomycin-oral.notes: Non-absorbed; CDI only. Monitor levels in severe colitis (systemic absorption rare but possible).

adult.bacteremia_ie: 6 mg/kg IV daily (8-10 mg/kg often used)

renal.dose: Standard dose q48h; HD: standard dose q48h post-HD

daptomycin.notes: INACTIVATED BY PULMONARY SURFACTANT — DO NOT USE FOR PNEUMONIA. Monitor CK weekly (myopathy).

linezolid.notes: Reversible cytopenias, peripheral & optic neuropathy with prolonged use, serotonin syndrome with serotonergic drugs (MAOI activity). 100% PO bioavailability.

tedizolid.notes: Less myelosuppression than linezolid in 6-day SSTI courses. SSTI-only FDA indication.

adult.default: 500 mg ×1 then 250 mg daily ×4 days (CAP) OR 500 mg daily ×3d

azithromycin.notes: QT prolongation. Pneumococcal macrolide resistance >25% in many regions — empiric use questionable.

adult.h_pylori: 500 mg BID with PPI + amox 1 g BID ×14d (clari triple therapy — declining use)

clarithromycin.notes: Many drug-drug interactions (CYP3A4 inhibition). H. pylori clarithromycin resistance >15% — bismuth quad preferred per ACG 2024.

pediatric.notes: AAP 2018: short courses (≤21d) safe in young children for Lyme, RMSF, etc.

doxycycline.pregnancy: D (avoid pregnancy ≥20w; AAP relaxed peds restriction)
doxycycline.notes: Photosensitivity. Take with water; do not lie down for 30 min (esophagitis). Do not take with calcium/iron/dairy concurrently.

renal.dose: Use cautiously; max 200 mg/d in renal impairment per label

minocycline.notes: Vestibular toxicity, hyperpigmentation, drug-induced lupus. Stenotrophomonas, CRAB combination.

adult.default: 100 mg IV ×1 then 50 mg IV q12h
adult.high_dose_crab: 200 mg ×1 then 100 mg q12h (off-label CRAB salvage)

tigecycline.hepatic: Reduce maintenance to 25 mg q12h in Child-Pugh C
tigecycline.notes: FDA boxed warning: increased mortality. NOT for bloodstream infections. Reserve for limited-option scenarios.

eravacycline.hepatic: Increase frequency in Child-Pugh C: 1 mg/kg q12h ×D1 then 1 mg/kg q24h
eravacycline.notes: cIAI. Active vs ESBL, CRE, CRAB. Strong inducer effect — drug interactions.

adult.ssti_load: 200 mg IV ×1 OR 450 mg PO ×2 days then 100 mg IV or 300 mg PO daily
adult.cabp_load: 200 mg IV ×1 then 100 mg IV daily; PO 300 mg daily after IV

omadacycline.notes: CABP, ABSSSI. Active vs MRSA, atypicals. Less GI than other tetracyclines.

adult.default: 500-750 mg PO BID OR 400 mg IV q8-12h

pediatric.notes: Reserve due to musculoskeletal AEs in peds

ciprofloxacin.notes: BBW: tendinopathy, peripheral neuropathy, CNS, aortic aneurysm. Avoid with antacids/Ca/Fe/Mg/Zn (chelation). QT prolongation.

levofloxacin.notes: Same FQ class warnings as ciprofloxacin. Better S. pneumoniae coverage; standard atypical coverage.

moxifloxacin.notes: Anaerobic coverage. NOT for UTI (no urinary excretion). QTc prolongation > other FQs.

adult.default: 300 mg IV q12h OR 450 mg PO q12h

renal.dose: 200 mg IV q12h or 450 mg PO q12h

delafloxacin.notes: MRSA-active FQ. SSTI, CABP. No QT prolongation.

renal.dose: Avoid; if used HD: half dose post-HD

trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole.notes: Hyperkalemia, AKI (pseudo or true), Stevens-Johnson, hyponatremia, sulfa allergy. Many drug interactions (warfarin, ACE-I, methotrexate, sulfonylureas).

clindamycin.notes: C. diff risk highest among common antibiotics. D-test required if isolate erythromycin-resistant + clindamycin-susceptible (inducible MLSb).

adult.trichomonas: 2 g PO ×1 OR 500 mg PO BID ×7d

metronidazole.notes: Disulfiram-like reaction with alcohol (controversial but stated on label). Anaerobic gram-negative + protozoa.

renal.dose: Avoid (efficacy concerns; some 2024 data suggest okay short course down to CrCl 20 — local guidance)

nitrofurantoin.notes: Cystitis only — no tissue penetration. NOT for pyelonephritis. Pulmonary toxicity, hepatotoxicity with long-term suppressive use. Avoid in G6PD.

fosfomycin.notes: Cystitis (E. coli, E. faecalis). NOT for pyelonephritis or systemic infections (PO formulation).

adult.extended_pulse: 200 mg BID days 1-5 then 200 mg every other day days 7-25 (recurrent CDI)

pediatric.weight_based_oral_susp: Refer to label by weight; 6 mo+ approved

fidaxomicin.notes: Preferred over vancomycin for initial AND recurrent CDI per IDSA 2021. Cost limits use.

adult.default: 150 mg IV q12h OR 600 mg PO q12h × 5 days (CABP)

lefamulin.hepatic: Use IV cautiously in Child-Pugh C; PO not recommended
lefamulin.notes: CABP including atypicals + MRSA. QT prolongation.

gepotidacin.notes: FDA approved March 2025 for uUTI in females ≥12y, ≥40 kg. Active vs E. coli, K. pneumoniae, S. saprophyticus, E. faecalis. QT prolongation.

adult.default: 500 mg/500 mg (sulopenem/probenecid) PO BID × 5 days (uncomplicated UTI)

sulopenem.notes: FDA approved Oct 2024 for uUTI caused by ESBL/FQ-resistant E. coli, K. pneumoniae, P. mirabilis. Women ≥18y.

zoliflodacin.notes: FDA approved 2025 for gonorrhea. Single oral dose alternative to ceftriaxone IM in ceftriaxone-resistant or allergic patients.

adult.extended_interval: 5-7 mg/kg IV q24h (use AdjBW if obese)

renal.dose: Dose by pharmacokinetic levels; HD: 1.5-2 mg/kg post-HD

gentamicin.notes: Nephrotoxicity, ototoxicity, vestibulotoxicity. Use AdjBW for obese (AdjBW = IBW + 0.4×(TBW-IBW)). Monitor levels.

tobramycin.notes: Slightly better Pseudomonas activity than gentamicin. Monitor levels.

amikacin.notes: Broader gram-negative including some MDR. Higher peak target than gent/tobra.

renal.dose: Half dose; HD: full dose post-HD

adult.maintenance: 4 mg/kg IV q12h OR 200 mg PO q12h

pediatric.mg_per_kg_per_dose: 9 IV load then 8 IV q12h or 9 PO q12h (2-12y)

renal.dose: Standard PO; AVOID IV (cyclodextrin) at CrCl<50
Use PO only or weigh IV cyclodextrin risk

voriconazole.hepatic: Halve maintenance dose in Child-Pugh A/B; avoid C unless benefit > risk
voriconazole.notes: Visual disturbances, hepatotoxicity, photosensitivity (skin cancer risk long-term), QT, many CYP3A4 interactions. Therapeutic drug monitoring required.

isavuconazole.hepatic: No adjustment Child-Pugh A/B; Child-Pugh C not studied
isavuconazole.notes: Aspergillosis, mucormycosis. SHORTENS QT (unlike other azoles). No cyclodextrin in IV. CYP3A4 interactions.

adult.prophylaxis_dr_tab: 300 mg PO BID ×D1 then 300 mg daily
adult.prophylaxis_susp: 200 mg PO TID with high-fat food
adult.iv: 300 mg IV BID ×D1 then 300 mg daily

posaconazole.notes: DR-tablet PK far superior to suspension. Many CYP3A4 interactions; pseudoaldosteronism / hypokalemia / HTN reported.

adult.default: 200 mg PO BID load ×3d then 200 mg PO BID-daily
adult.histo: 200 mg PO TID ×3d then 200 mg PO BID

itraconazole.notes: BBW: heart failure. Many CYP3A4 interactions. Solution requires fasting; capsules require food.

micafungin.notes: First-line candidemia. Limited oral bioavailability (IV only).

adult.maintenance: 50 mg IV daily (70 mg if >80 kg)

caspofungin.hepatic: Reduce maintenance to 35 mg in Child-Pugh B
caspofungin.notes: Candidemia, invasive aspergillosis salvage. Increase to 70 mg with rifampin co-admin.

adult.candidemia: 200 mg IV ×1 then 100 mg IV daily

anidulafungin.notes: No drug-drug interactions of clinical significance.

liposomal-amphotericin-b.notes: Less nephrotoxic and infusion-reactive than conventional ampho. Monitor K+, Mg2+, renal function.

pediatric.vzv_mg_per_kg_per_dose: 20 (max 800 mg) PO QID; 10 mg/kg IV q8h

acyclovir.weight_basis: actual (use IBW if obese to avoid AKI)

renal.dose: Half dose q24h; HD: dose post-HD

acyclovir.notes: Crystal nephropathy — hydrate well, infuse slowly. Use IBW for obese to limit AKI risk.

valacyclovir.notes: TTP/HUS reported in immunocompromised at very high doses.

oseltamivir.notes: Start within 48h ideal; CDC recommends in severe/hospitalized regardless of timing.

adult.default: 40 mg PO ×1 (40-80 kg) OR 80 mg PO ×1 (≥80 kg)

pediatric.dose: Per weight bands: 20 mg (<20 kg), 40 mg (20-<80 kg), 80 mg (≥80 kg)

baloxavir.notes: Single-dose. Avoid co-admin with dairy/Ca/Mg/Fe (chelation). I38T mutation can emerge — concern in immunocompromised.

adult.default: 300/100 mg PO BID ×5 days; start within 5 days of symptom onset

renal.dose: Avoid (per FDA label; some real-world data suggest reduced regimen feasible — defer to ID)

nirmatrelvir-ritonavir.notes: Massive drug-drug interactions via ritonavir CYP3A4 inhibition. CHECK before prescribing.
